reason: The remediation involves modifying kernel boot arguments via grubby or bootc kargs. While this is a standard hardening practice to prevent use-after-free vulnerabilities, changing kernel parameters carries an inherent risk of boot failure or kernel panics if the specific kernel version or hardware environment does not support the parameter or if the bootloader configuration is corrupted.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Potential for boot failure if kernel parameters are malformed; Kernel parameter changes require a reboot to take effect; Impact on system performance (initialization overhead)
